Definition: Free on Board. The seller delivers when the goods pass the ship's rail at the named port of shipment. This means the buyer bears all costs and risks of loss/damage to the goods from that point.
Purpose: To define the point of risk and cost transfer at the port of origin.
Common Problems: Disputes over loading damage or delay in nominating the vessel.

Definition: Cost, Insurance, and Freight. The seller pays the costs, insurance, and freight necessary to bring the goods to the named port of destination. However, the risk of loss or damage transfers to the buyer upon shipment.
Purpose: To bundle freight costs and marine insurance up to the destination port.
Common Problems: Disputes over insurance claims processing or cargo damage in transit.

Definition: A document issued by a carrier to acknowledge receipt of cargo for shipment. It serves as a contract of carriage, a receipt of goods, and a document of title.
Purpose: To act as evidence of the contract of carriage and transfer ownership of goods.
Common Problems: Discrepancies in weight or description; lost physical documents causing release delays.

Definition: A legal document issued by the seller to the buyer that serves as a bill for the sale of goods. It contains descriptions, unit values, quantities, and terms of sale.
Purpose: To serve as the primary document for customs valuation and duty calculation.
Common Problems: Incorrect HS codes or declared values leading to customs delays and fines.

Definition: A document detailing the contents, weight, volume, and dimensions of each package in a shipment.
Purpose: To allow cargo verification during shipping

Definition: Harmonized System Code. A standardized numerical method of classifying traded products, used by customs authorities worldwide to assess duties and apply regulations.
Purpose: To globally classify goods for tariff and statistical tracking.
Common Problems: Misclassification leading to incorrect duty payments

Definition: A letter from a bank guaranteeing that a buyer's payment to a seller will be received on time and for the correct amount, contingent on complying with specified conditions.
Purpose: To reduce credit risk for the seller by substituting the bank's creditworthiness for the buyer's.
Common Problems: Discrepancies in documents submitted to the bank
